warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
The fukui-reactivity analysis on nitrobenzene was performed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory (tier `standard`, density-fit) in PySCF. The calculation was performed in the gas phase with a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1. The geometry was taken from the provided `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz` file.

The most electrophilic site (highest f+) is Carbon atom 4 (index 3) with an f+ value of 0.49642.
The most nucleophilic site (highest f-) is Carbon atom 5 (index 4) with an f- value of 0.10729.

The Fukui indices and dual descriptor for each atom are as follows (atom_index_0based: f_plus, f_minus, f_zero, dual_descriptor):
- O (0): 0.47317, 0.09702, 0.28509, 0.37616
- O (1): 0.47344, 0.09677, 0.28511, 0.37667
- N (2): -0.45670, 0.01724, -0.21973, -0.47394
- C (3): 0.49642, -0.01429, 0.24106, 0.51071
- C (4): 0.11563, 0.10729, 0.11146, 0.00834
- C (5): 0.11588, 0.10724, 0.11156, 0.00865
- C (6): 0.17412, 0.09492, 0.13452, 0.07920
- C (7): 0.17423, 0.09489, 0.13456, 0.07934
- C (8): 0.28914, 0.00831, 0.14872, 0.28082
- H (9): -0.23169, 0.07521, -0.07824, -0.30690
- H (10): -0.23182, 0.07520, -0.07831, -0.30702
- H (11): -0.13372, 0.08388, -0.02492, -0.21761
- H (12): -0.13379, 0.08388, -0.02496, -0.21768
- H (13): -0.12431, 0.07243, -0.02594, -0.19673

The partial charges scheme used was Mulliken (PySCF).
The calculation was successful and trustworthy. The output JSON can be found at `nitrobenzene_fukui_dft.json`. A PNG bar chart of the f+/f-/dual values per atom is located at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/argo_gemini-2.5-flash/20260604-054059_nitrobenzene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.
